findings.description: The `@Published` annotation on `isSuccess()` is not reflected in the knowledge file. In the source, only `isSuccess()` carries `@Published`, meaning it is the sole method in the `Result` interface guaranteed for application-developer use. `getStatusCode()` and `getMessage()` are not `@Published`. This distinction is decision-necessary: an AI agent advising a developer on which `Result` methods are safe to call in business code would give incorrect guidance without it.
`@Published` is absent from the hints. It appears in the source as an annotation on `isSuccess()` and is a searchable marker for identifying Nablarch's public-API boundary.

`ExecutionContext` is missing from hints. It appears in both method signatures of `TransactionEventCallback` (`transactionNormalEnd(TData data, ExecutionContext ctx)` and `transactionAbnormalEnd(Throwable e, TData data, ExecutionContext ctx)`) and is a prominent class developers would search by name.
findings.source_evidence:
void transactionNormalEnd(TData data, ExecutionContext ctx);
void transactionAbnormalEnd(Throwable e, TData data, ExecutionContext ctx);

findings.description: `ExecutionContext` is missing from hints. It appears as a parameter in all three `DataReader` method signatures (`read`, `hasNext`, `close`) and is a class developers commonly search for when working with data readers.
